page: Microsoft Azure NC144lds_xl_RTXPRO6000BSE_v6 is a GPU-accelerated server instance belonging to the NCdsRTXPRO6000BSEv6XL family. It features 144 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture with an Intel processor, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 264.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.83 GB per vCPU.

This virtual machine includes a single integrated GPU and is equipped with 7921 GB of local storage. It is also Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments. The combination of a dedicated GPU and substantial local storage capacity supports workloads requiring fast local data access and hardware acceleration. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile of this instance prioritizes massive parallel CPU compute, GPU acceleration, and dense local storage over system memory capacity. This makes it a specialized option where resource allocation is optimized for tasks that do not require high memory-to-core ratios. Suitable workloads include GPU-accelerated compute tasks, local storage-heavy processing, and parallelized engineering simulations that fit within the 264.0 GB memory boundary.
description: The Microsoft Azure NC144lds_xl_RTXPRO6000BSE_v6 is a specialized, GPU-accelerated server instance from the NCdsRTXPRO6000BSEv6XL family. Powered by an Intel processor with x86_64 architecture, it provides 144 dedicated vCPUs managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The hardware configuration includes 264.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.83 GB per vCPU. For acceleration and data handling, the instance features a single integrated GPU and 7921 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallel compute and GPU-driven workloads that require substantial local storage but have modest system memory requirements. It is suited for GPU-accelerated simulations, local data processing, and highly parallelized compute tasks.
